instructions.instruction: Produce a handoff, never implementation code
instructions.original_snippets: **Never write implementation code in this skill.** Hand off to the specialist owner. ... This skill produces a handoff, not code.
instructions.relevant_when: Routing a PubNub question for the first time

instructions.instruction: Use the exact 5-line handoff template with Intent, Skill, MCP tool, Docs source, and Next step fields
instructions.original_snippets: When handing off, produce exactly one block in this shape: Intent      : <one sentence restating what the user is trying to do> Skill       : <specialist-skill-name> MCP tool    : <user-pubnub MCP tool> Docs source : <which PubNub docs surface to consult> Next step   : <one concrete action the user or agent should take next>
instructions.relevant_when: Producing any routing output

instructions.instruction: Always name both the specialist skill and the MCP tool in the handoff
instructions.original_snippets: **Always name both the skill and the MCP tool.** A handoff without both is incomplete.
instructions.relevant_when: Filling in the handoff template

instructions.instruction: Stop after one handoff; do not chain multiple handoffs
instructions.original_snippets: **Stop after one handoff.** If the user has multiple intents, ask which to address first.
instructions.relevant_when: Responding to any routing query

instructions.instruction: If the user has multiple intents, ask which to address first instead of guessing
instructions.original_snippets: **Stop after one handoff.** If the user has multiple intents, ask which to address first.
instructions.relevant_when: User question combines multiple distinct PubNub concerns

instructions.instruction: If the user has already named a specific feature, defer to the matching specialist directly instead of running this router
instructions.original_snippets: Do **not** invoke this skill when the user has already named a specific feature (chat, presence, illuminate, history, etc.) â€” load the matching specialist skill directly.
instructions.relevant_when: User explicitly names a PubNub feature/SDK in their question

instructions.instruction: Route chat/messaging/DM/typing/reactions/threads intents to pubnub-chat with get_chat_sdk_documentation
instructions.original_snippets: Build 1:1 DM, group chat, channels, message reactions, typing indicators | `pubnub-chat` | `get_chat_sdk_documentation` | Chat SDK docs
instructions.relevant_when: User describes a chat or messaging feature

instructions.instruction: Route non-chat real-time core SDK intents (IoT, telemetry, state sync, push fan-out) to pubnub-app-developer with get_sdk_documentation
instructions.original_snippets: IoT telemetry, device-to-cloud streaming | `pubnub-app-developer` | `get_sdk_documentation` | Core SDK docs ... Push notifications, fan-out broadcasts | `pubnub-app-developer` | `get_sdk_documentation` | Core SDK docs
instructions.relevant_when: User describes IoT, generic real-time core SDK use, or push fan-out

instructions.instruction: Route presence questions (online/offline, occupancy, multi-device sync) to pubnub-presence with get_pubnub_presence (runtime) or get_sdk_documentation (design)
instructions.original_snippets: Show online/offline indicators, room occupancy | `pubnub-presence` | `get_pubnub_presence` (runtime) + `get_sdk_documentation` | Presence docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about who is online, occupancy, or multi-device presence

instructions.instruction: Route history/replay/catch-up/timetoken-pagination intents to pubnub-history with get_pubnub_messages
instructions.original_snippets: Catch up an offline user, paginate by timetoken | `pubnub-history` | `get_pubnub_messages` + `get_sdk_documentation` | History/Storage docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about retrieving past messages, catch-up, or pagination

instructions.instruction: Route user/channel/membership metadata intents to pubnub-app-context with manage_app_context
instructions.original_snippets: Store user profile, avatar, role | `pubnub-app-context` | `manage_app_context` | Objects/App Context docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about user profiles, channel metadata, or membership metadata

instructions.instruction: Route per-message edge logic (transform, validate, enrich, moderate) to pubnub-functions, not pubnub-events-and-actions
instructions.original_snippets: **"Transform / validate / enrich the message inline"** â†’ `pubnub-functions` (not `pubnub-events-and-actions`).
instructions.relevant_when: User wants logic that runs on each PubNub message inline

instructions.instruction: Route 'forward to external system' (webhook, Lambda, Kafka, SQS, EventBridge) to pubnub-events-and-actions, not pubnub-functions
instructions.original_snippets: **"Send a message every time X happens" + external destination** â†’ `pubnub-events-and-actions` (not `pubnub-functions`). ... Forward every message to webhook / Lambda / Kafka / SQS / EventBridge | `pubnub-events-and-actions`
instructions.relevant_when: User wants to forward PubNub events to an external destination

instructions.instruction: Route analytics/KPI/threshold-alerts/dashboards intents to pubnub-illuminate with manage_illuminate
instructions.original_snippets: Track real-time KPIs (message counts, top users, session durations) | `pubnub-illuminate` | `manage_illuminate` | Illuminate docs ... Threshold-triggered actions (alert when X exceeds Y) | `pubnub-illuminate` | `manage_illuminate` | Illuminate docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about real-time KPIs, thresholds, anomaly detection, or dashboards

instructions.instruction: Route app/keyset/environment-separation intents to pubnub-keyset-management with manage_apps or manage_keysets
instructions.original_snippets: Create/inspect/update an app | `pubnub-keyset-management` | `manage_apps` | Admin Portal docs ... Create/inspect/update a keyset, separate dev/staging/prod | `pubnub-keyset-management` | `manage_keysets` | Admin Portal docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about creating apps, keysets, or separating environments

instructions.instruction: Route Access Manager / auth / encryption / TLS intents to pubnub-security with get_sdk_documentation
instructions.original_snippets: Authenticate clients, issue tokens, restrict channels | `pubnub-security` | `get_sdk_documentation` | Access Manager docs ... Encrypt messages or files | `pubnub-security` | `get_sdk_documentation` | Encryption docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about authentication, authorization, encryption, or TLS

instructions.instruction: Route channel-groups, wildcard subscribe, and scale-tuning intents to pubnub-scale with get_sdk_documentation
instructions.original_snippets: Channel groups, wildcard subscribe | `pubnub-scale` | `get_sdk_documentation` | Stream Controller docs ... Plan for 10k+ concurrent users / live event | `pubnub-scale` | `write_pubnub_app` | Best-practices docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about channel groups, wildcards, high throughput, or large live events

instructions.instruction: Route backoff, idempotency, retry, and dedup intents to pubnub-reliability with write_pubnub_app
instructions.original_snippets: Reliability patterns (backoff, idempotency, retry, dedup) | `pubnub-reliability` | `write_pubnub_app` | Best-practices docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about reconnect strategy, retry, dedup, or idempotent publishes

instructions.instruction: Route logging-correlation, test-pyramid, and runbook intents to pubnub-observability with write_pubnub_app
instructions.original_snippets: Logging correlation, test pyramid, runbook | `pubnub-observability` | `write_pubnub_app` | Best-practices docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about observability, logging, testing strategy, or runbooks

instructions.instruction: Route 'send a test message' runtime intent to send_pubnub_message MCP tool
instructions.original_snippets: Send a test message to validate publish | `pubnub-app-developer` | `send_pubnub_message` | n/a
instructions.relevant_when: User wants to publish a one-off message for testing or validation

instructions.instruction: Route 'subscribe and observe live messages' runtime intent to subscribe_and_receive_pubnub_messages MCP tool
instructions.original_snippets: Subscribe and observe live messages | `pubnub-app-developer` | `subscribe_and_receive_pubnub_messages` | n/a
instructions.relevant_when: User wants to live-subscribe to a channel from a test or runtime tool

instructions.instruction: Route conceptual or step-by-step multi-product recipes to the how_to MCP tool first, then hand off to the specialist owner
instructions.original_snippets: When the user asks "how do I X" with a step-by-step expectation and X spans multiple PubNub products, prefer the `how_to` MCP tool first to retrieve the relevant recipe, then hand off to the specialist skill that owns the implementation.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks an open-ended 'how do I' question that spans multiple PubNub products

instructions.instruction: Route architecture review / production-readiness intents to write_pubnub_app
instructions.original_snippets: Architecture review / production-readiness | `pubnub-reliability` + `pubnub-observability` + `pubnub-security` | `write_pubnub_app` | Best-practices docs
instructions.relevant_when: User asks for an architecture review or production-readiness check

instructions.instruction: Apply disambiguation heuristics: online indicator = presence, reconnect = reliability, 'was user here 10 min ago' = history
instructions.original_snippets: **"Online / offline indicator"** â†’ `pubnub-presence`. **"Reconnect after dropout"** â†’ `pubnub-reliability`. **"Was this user in the channel 10 min ago"** â†’ `pubnub-history`.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about presence-adjacent topics that could plausibly be presence, reliability, or history

instructions.instruction: User profile / avatar / role questions go to pubnub-app-context, even in a chat app
instructions.original_snippets: **"User profile, avatar, role"** â†’ `pubnub-app-context` (not `pubnub-chat`, even in a chat app).
instructions.relevant_when: User asks where user profile/avatar/role data should live

instructions.instruction: Never paraphrase another skill's content; link to it via the canonical owner file
instructions.original_snippets: **Never paraphrase another skill's content.** Link instead. The full mapping lives in [references/intent-to-tool.md](references/intent-to-tool.md).
instructions.relevant_when: Producing a handoff that mentions an owned topic (Access Manager, pagination, App Context, etc.)

instructions.instruction: Spam / chat-flooding / cross-posting anomaly detection routes to pubnub-illuminate, not pubnub-functions with custom logic
instructions.original_snippets: **"Track a KPI and alert when it crosses a threshold"** â†’ `pubnub-illuminate` (not `pubnub-functions` + custom logic). ... Anomaly detection (cross-posting spam, chat flooding) | `pubnub-illuminate`
instructions.relevant_when: User asks how to detect spam, flooding, or other anomalies in PubNub traffic
